                      [0-1898] link: Chain Abstraction with ZeroDev: Transaction Flows with Less Friction A workshop on using ZeroDev’s abstraction tooling to simplify signing, gas, and execution flows in user-facing apps. Best suited to teams building in the general track and looking to make onchain interactions feel lighter, smoother, and more intuitive. Speakers: Kunal Arora Kunal Arora

                          [0-1892] heading: Chain Abstraction with ZeroDev: Transaction Flows with Less Friction

                      [0-1924] link: Openfort + x402: Embedded Wallets for Seamless App Payments A practical session on using Openfort wallets alongside x402-style payment flows to support cleaner onboarding and smoother in-app transactions. Good for teams exploring checkout, access, and embedded wallet experiences that feel closer to modern consumer apps than traditional crypto products. Speakers: Joan Alavedra Joan Alavedra

                            [0-1935] heading: Building Consumer Apps on Arbitrum Without Exposing the Chain
                            [0-1936] paragraph
                              [0-2311] StaticText: Explore how Arbitrum can support better user experiences for trading, payments, and consumer crypto apps. This session will focus on performance, scalability, and how to pair strong infra with chain abstraction patterns.

                    [0-2865] div
                      [0-2707] div
                        [0-2666] div
                          [0-2651] heading: 🏆 Universal Accounts Track
                          [0-2652] div
                            [0-2649] image: Particle Network logo
                            [0-2650] span
                              [0-2927] StaticText: Supported by
                              [0-2928] StaticText: Particle Network
                        [0-2665] div
                          [0-2655] div
                            [0-2929] StaticText: 1st Place
                            [0-2930] StaticText: (
                            [0-2931] StaticText: $2,500
                            [0-2932] StaticText: )
                          [0-2658] div
                            [0-2933] StaticText: 2nd Place
                            [0-2934] StaticText: (
                            [0-2935] StaticText: $2,000
                            [0-2936] StaticText: )
                          [0-2661] div
                            [0-2937] StaticText: 3rd Prize
                            [0-2938] StaticText: (
                            [0-2939] StaticText: $1,500
                            [0-2940] StaticText: )
                      [0-2703] div
                        [0-2668] paragraph
                          [0-2941] StaticText: Build with Particle Network's Universal Accounts SDK in
                          [0-2670] strong
                            [0-2942] StaticText: EIP-7702 mode
                          [0-2943] StaticText: . The user's EOA becomes a chain-abstracted account in place—one login, one balance, transactions on any chain with any asset. No new address, no migration, no smart-account deployment. Just upgrade the EOA and ship.
                        [0-2672] paragraph
                          [0-2673] strong
                            [0-2944] StaticText: Requirements:
                        [0-2674] list
                          [0-2675] listitem
                            [0-2873] ListMarker: •
                            [0-2945] StaticText: Must use Universal Accounts SDK in EIP-7702 mode.
                          [0-2677] listitem
                            [0-2874] ListMarker: •
                            [0-2946] StaticText: At least one cross-chain operation moving value via UA.
                          [0-2679] listitem
                            [0-2875] ListMarker: •
                            [0-2947] StaticText: Functional demo (deployed or runnable locally).
                        [0-2681] heading: Useful Links
                          [0-2682] strong
                            [0-2948] StaticText: Useful Links
                        [0-2683] list
                          [0-2684] listitem
                            [0-2876] ListMarker: •
                            [0-2685] strong
                              [0-2949] StaticText: Particle Developer Docs:
                            [0-2950] StaticText: 
                            [0-2686] link: https://developers.particle.network/
                          [0-2687] listitem
                            [0-2877] ListMarker: •
                            [0-2688] strong
                              [0-2952] StaticText: Universal Accounts Overview:
                            [0-2953] StaticText: 
                            [0-2689] link: https://developers.particle.network/universal-accounts/cha/overview
                          [0-2690] listitem
                            [0-2878] ListMarker: •
                            [0-2691] strong
                              [0-2955] StaticText: Web Quickstart:
                            [0-2956] StaticText: 
                            [0-2692] link: https://developers.particle.network/universal-accounts/cha/web-quickstart
                          [0-2693] listitem
                            [0-2879] ListMarker: •
                            [0-2694] strong
                              [0-2958] StaticText: Core concepts and chain-agnostic magic
                            [0-2959] StaticText: -
                            [0-2696] link: Introduction to Universal Accounts
                          [0-2698] listitem
                            [0-2880] ListMarker: •
                            [0-2699] strong
                              [0-2961] StaticText: Quickstart, integration guides, and 7702 mode details
                            [0-2962] StaticText: -
                            [0-2701] link: Universal Accounts SDK & EIP-7702 Overview
                        [0-2702] paragraph
                          [0-2964] StaticText: Projects selected as winners in this track may also be considered for incubation by Particle Network.
                    [0-2866] div
                      [0-2748] div
                        [0-2726] div
                          [0-2711] heading: 🏆 General Track
                          [0-2712] div
                            [0-2709] image: Particle Network logo
                            [0-2710] span
                              [0-2966] StaticText: Supported by
                              [0-2967] StaticText: Particle Network
                        [0-2725] div
                          [0-2715] div
                            [0-2968] StaticText: 1st Place
                            [0-2969] StaticText: (
                            [0-2970] StaticText: $2,000
                            [0-2971] StaticText: )
                          [0-2718] div
                            [0-2972] StaticText: 2nd Place
                            [0-2973] StaticText: (
                            [0-2974] StaticText: $1,200
                            [0-2975] StaticText: )
                          [0-2721] div
                            [0-2976] StaticText: 3rd Place
                            [0-2977] StaticText: (
                            [0-2978] StaticText: $800
                            [0-2979] StaticText: )
                      [0-2749] div
                        [0-2735] div
                          [0-2728] paragraph
                            [0-2980] StaticText: Build a Web3 application with exceptional user experience in any domain.
                          [0-2729] paragraph
                            [0-2981] StaticText: The General Track is open to teams creating consumer-friendly apps across payments, DeFi, AI, gaming, social, and beyond - as long as the experience is seamless, intuitive, and genuinely improved by the infrastructure being used.
                          [0-2730] paragraph
                            [0-2982] StaticText: Projects in the
                            [0-2731] strong
                              [0-2983] StaticText: General Track
                            [0-2984] StaticText: may also be eligible for additional prizes through their chosen subtrack, as well as the Arbitrum and Magic Labs bonus challenges.
                          [0-2732] paragraph
                            [0-2985] StaticText: All winning projects in this track may, at Particle Network’s discretion, be considered for incubation.
                          [0-2733] paragraph
                            [0-2986] StaticText: Projects selected as winners in this track may also be considered for incubation by Particle Network.Teams entering this track must
                            [0-2734] strong
                              [0-2987] StaticText: choose one of two subtracks:
                        [0-2747] div
                          [0-2744] heading: Tracks
                          [0-2745] div
                            [0-2742] div
                              [0-2989] StaticText: Subtrack 2 - ZeroDev
                              [0-2990] StaticText: $500
                            [0-2743] div
                              [0-2991] StaticText: Subtrack 1 - Openfort
                              [0-2992] StaticText: $100
                    [0-2867] div
                      [0-2796] div
                        [0-2759] div
                          [0-2752] heading: Arbitrum “Road to Open House London” Bounty
                          [0-2753] div
                            [0-2750] image: Arbitrum logo
                            [0-2751] span
                              [0-2994] StaticText: Supported by
                              [0-2995] StaticText: Arbitrum
                        [0-2756] div
                          [0-2996] StaticText: Prize pool
                          [0-2997] StaticText: (
                          [0-2998] StaticText: $2,000
                          [0-2999] StaticText: )
                      [0-2792] div
                        [0-2761] paragraph
                          [0-3000] StaticText: Leading up to Arbitrum's flagship Founder House residency hosted by Encode House in London, Arbitrum
                          [0-3001] StaticText: will independently award the best dApp in this hackathon with a single $2,000 prize, as long as the application and its components are deployed on the Arbitrum network.
                        [0-2763] paragraph
                          [0-3002] StaticText: Build a consumer app where Arbitrum powers the experience behind the scenes, but the user never has to think about wallets, gas fees, bridges, or what chain they’re using.
                        [0-2764] paragraph
                          [0-3003] StaticText: Projects must run primarily on Arbitrum and should use chain-abstracted UX patterns like embedded wallets, social login, gas abstraction, invisible bridging, or account abstraction.
                        [0-2765] heading: Example ideas:
                        [0-2766] list
                          [0-2767] listitem
                            [0-2881] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3005] StaticText: AI apps with invisible onchain payments
                          [0-2768] listitem
                            [0-2882] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3006] StaticText: Social or gaming apps with walletless onboarding
                          [0-2769] listitem
                            [0-2883] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3007] StaticText: Consumer payment or loyalty apps
                          [0-2770] listitem
                            [0-2884] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3008] StaticText: Cross-chain apps where Arbitrum acts as the backend settlement layer
                          [0-2771] listitem
                            [0-2885] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3009] StaticText: Mobile-first apps with seamless UX
                        [0-2772] heading: Judges will look for:
                        [0-2773] list
                          [0-2774] listitem
                            [0-2886] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3011] StaticText: UX excellence (30%)
                          [0-2775] listitem
                            [0-2887] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3012] StaticText: Creativity (30%)
                          [0-2776] listitem
                            [0-2888] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3013] StaticText: Adoption potential (20%)
                          [0-2777] listitem
                            [0-2889] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3014] StaticText: Execution quality (20%).
                        [0-2778] paragraph
                          [0-3015] StaticText: Judges may use the
                          [0-2779] strong
                            [0-3016] StaticText: overall app quality as a tie-breaker
                        [0-2780] paragraph
                          [0-3017] StaticText: The best projects will feel less like “crypto apps” and more like normal consumer products that just happen to run onchain.
                        [0-2781] paragraph
                          [0-2782] link: Arbitrum Developer Docs
                          [0-3019] StaticText: – Everything for deployment, bridging, and building on Arbitrum.
                        [0-2783] heading: Next step: Arbitrum Founder House London
                        [0-2784] paragraph
                          [0-3021] StaticText: Strong submissions are encouraged to apply for
                          [0-2785] strong
                            [0-3022] StaticText: Arbitrum's Founder House in London
                          [0-3023] StaticText: , an in-person founder program taking place from
                          [0-2786] strong
                            [0-3024] StaticText: July 10 to 12 in London
                          [0-3025] StaticText: .
                        [0-2787] paragraph
                          [0-3026] StaticText: The Founder House is designed for teams building serious onchain businesses on Arbitrum. Selected teams receive focused mentorship across product, technical direction, business strategy, and go-to-market, with the goal of accelerating participating founders.
                        [0-2788] paragraph
                          [0-3027] StaticText: The Founder House is application-based, with strictly limited capacity.
                        [0-2789] paragraph
                          [0-2790] link: Apply to the London Founder House
                            [0-2791] strong
                              [0-3028] StaticText: Apply to the London Founder House
                    [0-2868] div
                      [0-2833] div
                        [0-2807] div
                          [0-2800] heading: Magic Labs Bonus Challenge
                          [0-2801] div
                            [0-2798] image: Magic Labs logo
                            [0-2799] span
                              [0-3030] StaticText: Supported by
                              [0-3031] StaticText: Magic Labs
                        [0-2804] div
                          [0-3032] StaticText: Prize
                          [0-3033] StaticText: (
                          [0-3034] StaticText: $500
                          [0-3035] StaticText: )
                      [0-2829] div
                        [0-2809] paragraph
                          [0-3036] StaticText: Integrate Magic’s embedded wallet within your application and qualify for a single $500 prize for the wallet’s best and most creative implementation.
                        [0-2810] paragraph
                          [0-3037] StaticText: Build the best user onboarding and wallet experience using Magic’s embedded wallet infrastructure.
                        [0-2811] paragraph
                          [0-3038] StaticText: This prize is open to projects from any main track and is focused on creating seamless, low-friction UX for real users. Think social login, invisible wallets, smooth authentication, and apps that feel more like modern consumer products than traditional crypto apps.
                        [0-2812] heading: Example ideas:
                        [0-2813] list
                          [0-2814] listitem
                            [0-2890] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3040] StaticText: Walletless onboarding flows
                          [0-2815] listitem
                            [0-2891] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3041] StaticText: AI or social apps with embedded accounts
                          [0-2816] listitem
                            [0-2892] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3042] StaticText: Consumer apps with email or Google login
                          [0-2817] listitem
                            [0-2893] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3043] StaticText: Mobile-first onboarding experiences
                          [0-2818] listitem
                            [0-2894] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3044] StaticText: Apps where users never need to install MetaMask
                        [0-2819] heading: Judges will look for:
                        [0-2820] list
                          [0-2821] listitem
                            [0-2895] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3046] StaticText: Smooth onboarding and authentication
                          [0-2822] listitem
                            [0-2896] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3047] StaticText: Creative use of Magic infrastructure
                          [0-2823] listitem
                            [0-2897] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3048] StaticText: UX polish and accessibility
                          [0-2824] listitem
                            [0-2898] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3049] StaticText: Consumer-ready product thinking
                          [0-2825] listitem
                            [0-2899] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3050] StaticText: Technical implementation quality
                        [0-2826] paragraph
                          [0-2827] link: Magic Embedded Wallets Documentation
                          [0-3052] StaticText: – Seamless onboarding, auth, and multi-chain support.
                    [0-2869] div
                      [0-2840] div
                        [0-2835] heading: Track Selection, Rules & Judging
                        [0-2839] div
                          [0-2836] image: Particle Network logo
                          [0-2837] link: Supported by Particle Network
                            [0-3054] StaticText: Supported by
                            [0-3055] StaticText: Particle Network
                      [0-2861] div
                        [0-2842] heading: Participants must submit their project to one main track:
                        [0-2843] paragraph
                          [0-2844] strong
                            [0-3057] StaticText: Universal Accounts Track:
                          [0-3058] StaticText: Build a dApp that prominently uses Particle Network’s Universal Accounts in EIP-7702 mode, in combination with a supported wallet provider, for a chain-agnostic UX.
                        [0-2845] paragraph
                          [0-3059] StaticText: Or
                          [0-2846] strong
                            [0-3060] StaticText: General Track:
                          [0-3061] StaticText: 
                          [0-3062] StaticText: Build a Web3 application with exceptional user experience in any domain.
                        [0-2848] paragraph
                          [0-3063] StaticText: Projects on this track, regardless of their track-winning status, can also earn additional bonus prizes through the Arbitrum ($2000) and Magic Labs ($500) bounties. These bounties will be independently judged and awarded and, as such, will follow their own criteria and standards.
                        [0-2849] paragraph
                          [0-3064] StaticText: Besides the two main tracks and the General track’s subtracks, there are two additional bounties that participants can choose to pursue, regardless of their track choice.
                        [0-2851] paragraph
                          [0-3065] StaticText: All winning projects in this track may, at Particle Network's discretion, be selected for incubation.
                        [0-2852] heading: Criteria
                        [0-2853] paragraph
                          [0-3067] StaticText: Judges will seek the most seamless user experience, with particular emphasis on chain-agnostic features powered by Universal Accounts + EIP-7702. They will focus on UX excellence (40%), Prominent/innovative use of Universal Accounts + EIP-7702 (30%), adoption potential (20%), and technical quality/polish (10%).
                        [0-2854] heading: Judging note:
                        [0-2855] paragraph
                          [0-3069] StaticText: Main track winners will be selected by the hackathon jury, while subtracks and bonus challenges will be judged independently by the relevant partner teams. Teams may therefore be considered for multiple prizes depending
                        [0-2856] heading: Rules:
                        [0-2857] list
                          [0-2858] listitem
                            [0-2900] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3071] StaticText: All work original and created during the hackathon. Plagiarism = disqualification.
                          [0-2859] listitem
                            [0-2901] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3072] StaticText: Judges’ decisions are final.
                          [0-2860] listitem
                            [0-2902] ListMarker: •
                            [0-3073] StaticText: You keep full IP rights.
